opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This throbbing in my jaw is driving me insane, and now my cheek is ballooning up so much I can barely open my mouth.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Severe tooth pain with rapidly worsening facial swelling and inability to open the mouth fully.

symptom_narrative.onset: throbbing tooth pain for 48 hours, facial swelling and jaw stiffness over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: throbbing localized tooth pain with progressive facial swelling
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: unbearable, refractory to maximum over-the-counter painkillers
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right lower jaw, spreading to the cheek
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: ice pack helps slightly with pain, nothing else
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to open the mouth or chew
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: bad taste in the mouth, feeling generally unwell and feverish, unable to open mouth more than about 2cm
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: unable to eat or drink normally, pacing with an ice pack on the cheek

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: taken repeatedly, exceeding the recommended daily dose trying to control the pain

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse present and concerned, no regular dental check-ups in recent years

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: desperate for pain relief and wants to avoid an ED visit if possible
